name: LangChain
tagline: The agent framework that outlived the hype cycle
description: The original LLM application framework, now consolidated around durable agent runtimes: abstractions for tools, retrieval, memory, and multi-step agents across every major model provider, in Python and JavaScript.
why: Still the most-adopted way to build agents, and its 1.x consolidation around LangGraph marks the ecosystem's real shift — from prompt chains to long-running, stateful agent systems.
useCases: Standardizing LLM plumbing across a team
audience: Application developers; enterprises standardizing an agent stack

setup.requirements: Python 3.9+ or Node; API keys for your model providers. Easy to install, sizeable API surface to master.

license.notes: The framework is MIT; LangSmith, the hosted observability product, is proprietary SaaS.

limitations: Abstraction-heavy by design, with a history of API churn between major versions. Learn the primitives before adopting the whole tower.
take: Not the newest thing in the room anymore — which is exactly why it's safe to build on.
